sections.paragraph:
ψοφοδεής, ές (ψόφος, δέος; Pla. et al.; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 32 p. 215 ln. 15) the lit. sense ‘pert. to being easily frightened’ transfers to timid, anxious the neut. as subst. τὸ ψοφοδεές timidity, anxiety (Plut., Comp. Nic. cum Crass. 566 [2, 4], Nic. 524 [2, 6]) Dg 4:1.—DELG s.v. ψόφος.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. ln. = line

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1098
